title: Correct: unclosed delimiters produce clean errors, not panics
description: WHY THIS IS CORRECT: read_string_list, read_test_list, and read_block all check pos >= tokens.len() at the top of their loop and return descriptive ParseErrors before any index operation. Unclosed '[', '(', and '{' at any nesting level produce clean errors. The lexer similarly handles unterminated quoted strings and unterminated block comments with explicit EOF checks before advancing. No panic path exists for malformed-but-finite input in the delimiter handling.
